html:
MOTHER.BK Thesis

MOTHER is not a marketing-services company. The name is misleading. The actual business is a tiny southern Thailand grocery and wholesale retailer operating Mother Supermarket and Mother Marche stores in Krabi, Phang Nga, Surat Thani, and nearby provinces. That wrong label matters because the stock is being ignored as a sleepy post-IPO microcap, while the operating model is starting to show branch-level scale and profit leverage.

At THB 1.15, MOTHER is valued at about THB 329 million, or roughly 12.3x trailing earnings. That is not obviously cheap on stale FY2025 net profit of THB 24 million. The forward case is different. Q1 2026 revenue rose 6.4% to THB 418.7 million, EBIT rose 20.1%, and net profit rose 27.8% to THB 12.6 million, with gross margin improving to 13.49% from 12.90% and finance cost falling 36% as IPO proceeds and cash flow reduced debt. Annualizing Q1 is too aggressive because Q1 is seasonally strong, but it does show earnings power above the trailing run rate. A reasonable FY2026 profit range is THB 38-48 million, putting the stock on 6.9-8.7x forward earnings.

The variant perception is simple: MOTHER is still treated like a low-margin local grocer that came public at THB 1.40 and failed to hold its IPO price. In reality, it is a store-rollout story with visible unit expansion, tourism exposure, improving mix toward direct retail, lower interest expense, and unusually large upside from small absolute earnings gains. FY2025 same-store sales grew 7.23%, retail revenue rose 14.15%, and the company reached 22 branches. Q1 2026 still showed positive same-store sales growth of 1.91% even against a tougher high-season comparison, while retail sales rose 9.54% and the retail/wholesale mix moved to 74/26.

The upside torque comes from the branch math. Management has discussed adding seven branches with roughly THB 50 million of investment and targeting double-digit revenue growth. That is plausible rather than promotional. FY2025 sales were THB 1.43 billion across 22 branches, and two branches opened in 2025 contributed only partial-year revenue. In Q1 2026, those newer branches already added THB 21.8 million of sales, while existing branches added another THB 5.0 million. If new stores mature toward THB 45-60 million of annual sales each, MOTHER can add THB 250-350 million of revenue over the next two years without needing heroic assumptions.

The operating leverage is modest but real. A supermarket with 13% gross margins and 2% net margins does not need SaaS-like economics to rerate from a THB 329 million market cap. Moving from FY2025 net margin of 1.67% to 2.5-3.0% on THB 1.6-1.8 billion of sales gets to THB 40-54 million of net income. Q1 already printed a 3.03% net margin, helped by better gross margin and lower interest cost. The thesis is that MOTHER does not need a structural margin miracle. It needs revenue density, working-capital discipline, and enough supplier scale to keep gross margin near 13.3-13.7%.

The local setup helps. Krabi was one of the top domestic Songkran destinations in Traveloka’s 2026 travel data, and MOTHER’s store base is directly exposed to tourist-zone consumption rather than generic Bangkok retail. Thailand’s overall tourist arrivals are not booming, but MOTHER does not need national tourism acceleration. It needs spending in its southern catchment to remain firm while new stores mature. The government’s Thai Help Thai Plus / welfare-card stimulus is also relevant because it targets mass-market consumption through existing participating stores, exactly the kind of basket MOTHER serves.

Capital allocation is no longer a zero. The company declared a THB 0.034 dividend, ex-date May 5, 2026 and payable May 22, implying roughly a 3% yield at today’s price. That is not a special dividend and not a cash-unlock event, but it matters because it establishes that minorities are receiving cash while the business is still funding expansion. The balance sheet is not a hidden net-cash fortress. SET shows EV above market cap, and Q1 liabilities were THB 284 million against equity of THB 278 million. The good news is not “cash floor.” The good news is falling finance cost, improving equity base, and a business that can finance small-format growth without blowing up leverage.

The bull case is that MOTHER becomes recognized as a southern Thailand version of a regional grocery compounder. The market does not need to pay 25x earnings. If FY2027 earnings reach THB 55-60 million and the stock is valued at 11-13x because same-store sales stay positive, new stores are productive, and dividend payments continue, equity value can be THB 600-780 million. That is THB 2.10-2.70 per share.

The main risks are clear. Gross margin is thin, so supplier inflation, price controls, and promotions can erase profit quickly. Tourism is a tailwind but also a cyclicality source. Wholesale sales are shrinking, which is acceptable only if retail growth keeps replacing them at better contribution margins. Liquidity and governance are real constraints, with roughly 70% insider ownership and a tiny institutional footprint. The stock can stay cheap simply because nobody large can buy it.

Still, the honest forward thesis is attractive: not a fantasy special dividend story, not an AI pivot, but a THB 329 million regional grocer with visible store growth, improving Q1 earnings, lower finance cost, and a credible path to THB 40-55 million of annual profit. That makes the stock cheap enough for the risk if the branch rollout remains disciplined.

Bear target: THB 0.85, down 26%. This assumes revenue growth fades, FY2026 profit falls back toward THB 24-28 million, and the market values the stock at 9x earnings with no rollout credit.

Base target: THB 1.65, up 43%. This assumes FY2026 net profit of about THB 42 million and a 11x multiple as the market starts valuing MOTHER on forward earnings rather than FY2025.

Bull target: THB 2.50, up 117%. This assumes FY2027 earnings power of THB 55-60 million, continued positive same-store sales, successful new-branch maturation, and a 12x earnings multiple for a small but proven regional retailer.
